briefing:
## 🌍 Top 5 Global Fintech News

- **[Fintech founder charged with fraud; AI app found to be humans in the Philippines](https://techcrunch.com/2025/04/10/fintech-founder-charged-with-fraud-after-ai-shopping-app-found-to-be-powered-by-humans-in-the-philippines/)** — *Hacker News* — A fintech founder faces fraud charges after their supposedly AI-powered shopping app was revealed to be operated by human workers in the Philippines.

- **[Americans see their savings vanish in Synapse fintech crisis](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/11/22/synapse-bankruptcy-thousands-of-americans-see-their-savings-vanish.html)** — *Hacker News* — Thousands of Americans lost access to their savings following the collapse of fintech middleware provider Synapse, highlighting systemic risks in banking-as-a-service models.

- **[Fintech dystopia](https://fintechdystopia.com/)** — *Hacker News* — A critical look at the darker consequences of fintech's rapid growth and its impact on consumers and financial systems.

- **[Apple acquires UK open banking startup Credit Kudos](https://ffnews.com/newsarticle/apple-acquires-uk-open-banking-startup-credit-kudos/)** — *Hacker News* — Apple has acquired UK-based open banking startup Credit Kudos, signalling the tech giant's deeper ambitions in financial data and credit services.

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News* — An in-depth analysis of how open banking frameworks are reshaping competition in the payments landscape globally.

---

## 🍁 Top 5 Canadian Fintech News

- **[Global payroll platform Borderless AI now lets employees get paid in crypto](https://betakit.com/global-payroll-platform-borderless-ai-now-lets-employees-get-paid-in-crypto/)** — *BetaKit* — Canadian-linked global payroll platform Borderless AI has added cryptocurrency payment options, expanding how employers can compensate workers across borders.

- **[BDC's new $500-million loan program will help smaller businesses adopt AI](https://betakit.com/bdcs-new-500-million-loan-program-will-help-smaller-businesses-adopt-ai/)** — *BetaKit* — The Business Development Bank of Canada is launching a $500-million loan program to help small and medium-sized businesses integrate artificial intelligence into their operations.

- **[Cohere to acquire Germany's Aleph Alpha in sovereign AI play](https://betakit.com/cohere-to-acquire-germanys-aleph-alpha-in-sovereign-ai-play/)** — *BetaKit* — Toronto-based AI company Cohere is acquiring German AI firm Aleph Alpha in a move that strengthens its position in the sovereign AI market.

- **[Canada calls screen scraping 'unsecure,' sets Open Banking target for 2023](https://finledger.com/2021/08/09/canada-sets-open-banking-target-for-2023/)** — *Hacker News* — Canada's federal government flagged screen scraping as a security risk and set a formal open banking implementation target, a foundational moment for Canada's ongoing consumer-directed finance journey.

- **[U.S. soldier involved in Maduro capture accused of profiting from knowledge of raid on Polymarket](https://www.cbc.ca/news/world/us-soldier-charged-polymarket-maduro-bets-9.7175491)** — *CBC Business* — A U.S. soldier has been charged with using insider knowledge of a military operation to place profitable bets on prediction market platform Polymarket, raising serious questions about financial regulation of crypto-based prediction markets.

---

## 🌊 Bonus: Relevant to Atlantic Fintech's Focus (1–3 articles)

- **[Alberta establishes new office to spur on IP development](https://betakit.com/alberta-establishes-new-office-to-spur-on-ip-development/)** — *BetaKit* — Alberta's new IP development office offers a model worth watching for Atlantic Canadian ecosystem builders looking to turn regional innovation into commercially protected, investable assets.

- **[Fintech is making credit cards weirder](https://workweek.com/2022/08/05/fintech-is-making-credit-cards-weirder/)** — *Hacker News* — Fintech-driven innovation is transforming credit card products with novel rewards, structures, and embedded features — trends highly relevant to Atlantic Canadian credit unions exploring competitive product offerings.
